raw: {"weights": {"R1.1": 0.7, "R1.2": 0.3, "R1.3": 0.3, "R1.4": 0.7, "R1.5": 0.3, "R1.6": 0.7, "R1.7": 0.0, "RX.1": 1.0, "RX.2": 1.0, "RX.3": 1.0, "RX.4": 1.0, "RX.5": 1.0}, "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.4", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"], "rationale": {"R1.1": "The contribution claim and problem framing exist but are dispersed across paragraphs; tightening the thesis and gap statement is a plausible load-bearing interpretation of 'stronger'.", "R1.4": "The differentiation from constrained-decoding and post-editing prior work is underdeveloped; sharpening the novelty argument is a second plausible load-bearing interpretation.", "R1.6": "Sentence-level rhetoric and persuasive force are the most surface-obvious target of 'make stronger'; prose polish is a third plausible load-bearing interpretation."}, "clarification_needed": true, "clarification_question": "Should I focus primarily on prose clarity and rhetorical force (sentence/paragraph rewriting), or on sharpening the novelty argument and contribution claims relative to prior work?"}

rationale.R1.1: The contribution claim and problem framing exist but are dispersed across paragraphs; tightening the thesis and gap statement is a plausible load-bearing interpretation of 'stronger'.
rationale.R1.4: The differentiation from constrained-decoding and post-editing prior work is underdeveloped; sharpening the novelty argument is a second plausible load-bearing interpretation.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence-level rhetoric and persuasive force are the most surface-obvious target of 'make stronger'; prose polish is a third plausible load-bearing interpretation.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus primarily on prose clarity and rhetorical force (sentence/paragraph rewriting), or on sharpening the novelty argument and contribution claims relative to prior work?
